name: Python Library
description: You step into an enormous library with towering bookshelves stretching to a vaulted ceiling. Each shelf is labeled with an ecosystem name — numpy, scipy, torch, sklearn, matplotlib — their bindings glowing with import statements. The air smells of Jupyter notebooks and freshly brewed data. A grand staircase leads to the Eisenstein Gallery above.
look_text: The library is organized by integration depth. Ground floor: pure Python wrappers calling Fortran/C via ctypes and cffi. Mezzanine: ML pipeline integration, snap as a preprocessing step in sklearn transformers. Upper gallery: data science workflows, pandas DataFrames flowing through snap transformations. A terminal in the corner runs a PLATO API server, its endpoints humming with RESTful energy.

exits.description: The optimization chamber, where the numerical engines live
The lattice gallery, where the math becomes visual
The cathedral below, where XOR reveals truth

recipes.name: sklearn snap transformer
recipes.description: A scikit-learn compatible transformer that applies Eisenstein snap as a preprocessing step. Fits into any sklearn Pipeline. GPU optional via torch.

recipes.name: PLATO REST API
recipes.description: A FastAPI server exposing PLATO tile query, room navigation, and snap computation as REST endpoints. OpenAPI spec auto-generated.
